summary: Build chunk.py orchestrator (reads raw/, applies configs, writes corpus/)
description:
## Goal

Build the `chunk.py` orchestrator that walks `raw/`, applies the per-text chunking config, and writes the canonical TOML chunk files.

## File Location

`scripts/chunk.py`

## Responsibilities

1. For each `raw/{tradition}/{text_id}.txt`, load `chunking/{tradition}/{text_id}.toml`.
2. Dispatch to the splitter named by `[chunking].strategy` (regex / heading / paragraph).
3. Run each chunk through the token counter (ticket b019620d) and split if >`max_tokens`.
4. Write `corpus/{tradition}/{text_id}/chunks/{NNN}.toml` per chunk (schema below).
5. Write `corpus/{tradition}/{text_id}/metadata.toml` with text-level metadata.
6. Be idempotent — re-runs produce identical output.

## Chunk File Schema

```toml
[chunk]
id = "{tradition}.{text_id}.{NNN}"
tradition = "..."
text_name = "..."
section = "Logion 77"
translator = "..."
source_url = "..."
token_count = 62

[content]
body = """..."""

[annotations]
concepts = []
related_chunks = []
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every raw text produces at least one chunk file
- Chunk IDs are globally unique
- All required metadata fields are populated
- Idempotent across re-runs

resolution.note: scripts/chunk.py: walks raw/, dispatches regex/heading/paragraph splitters, fills token counts, sub-splits oversized chunks, writes corpus/{tradition}/{text_id}/chunks/{NNN}.toml + metadata.toml. Tested: 114 logion chunks for gospel-of-thomas, 6 section chunks for sefer-yetirah.
